summary: All four reviewers (reality, architecture, quality, systems) converge on CHANGES_REQUESTED. Five reality-verified blockers prove the plan's fixtures and allowlist do not match the codebase as-shipped (NodeSpec and OutputSpec fixtures TypeError on construction; 3 of 4 EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS plugin names are phantom; api/preferences.ts is a phantom precedent; No-Legacy policy is violated by deferring call-site updates). On top of those, four convergence findings — each flagged by 2-3 independent reviewers — confirm two architectural blockers (handleValidate deletion ahead of Phase 3A; affected_nodes producer never wired in validation.py), one correctness blocker (loadSnapshot stale-fetch race with no monotonic write guard), and one wiring decision required (secret_service vs scoped_secret_resolver). Quality adds an absent mypy/ruff convention gate. The structural approach is sound — TDD-shaped, clean L3 layering, sensible A/B/C split, defensible closed-allowlist trust classification — but the plan ships in its current state with broken fixtures, a known-empty provenance field, and a guaranteed regression window for validation chat feedback. Fix in-session; do not start implementation.

convergence_findings.title: affected_nodes producer not wired in validation.py — provenance row's jump-to-component is dead on arrival
convergence_findings.evidence: src/elspeth/web/execution/validation.py:1243-1254 constructs ValidationCheck(name=_CHECK_IDENTITY_NODE_ADVISORY, passed=True, detail=...) with NO affected_nodes kwarg. Plan adds affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = () to the schema but does not co-update the producer. Unit test in test_service.py supplies affected_nodes=('pass',) manually, masking the gap. CLAUDE.md No-Legacy policy: 'Change all call sites in the same commit.'
convergence_findings.resolution: In Phase 2A Task 3 (or a new Task 2.5), update validation.py around line 1248 to pass affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,). Add an integration test that exercises the real validate_pipeline path and asserts component_ids is populated in the provenance row. Same applies to ValidationError.error_code — update all construction sites that should carry an error code.

convergence_findings.title: loadSnapshot stale-fetch race — slow v=1 response overwrites fast v=2 with no monotonic write guard
convergence_findings.evidence: 14b auditReadinessStore.ts lines 678-700: version-keyed cache short-circuit fires only post-resolve. Two concurrent loadSnapshot calls for the same sessionId at versions 1 and 2 both see empty cache, both fetch, and the late resolver wins. Response carries composition_version but it is not compared against current state before set(). For an audit surface, 'Audit ready ✓' against a stale composition is a correctness failure, not a UX glitch.
convergence_findings.confidence: HIGH (3 reviewers; architecture also flagged the absence in the plan's risk table)
convergence_findings.resolution: Add a monotonic write guard in the resolution arm of loadSnapshot: before calling set(), compare the response's composition_version against the current cached version; discard if lower. Independently, add AbortController-based cancellation on version change and on clearSession (recommended by systems R4 and quality WARN-2). 5-line change; tests must cover the concurrent-fetch case (currently absent).

convergence_findings.title: handleValidate deletion before Phase 3A Task 8 lands — guaranteed regression window for validation-driven chat feedback
convergence_findings.evidence: 14c Task 8 deletes handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback from InspectorPanel.tsx claiming 'Phase 3A Task 8 already does this' relocation into subscriptions.ts. Verified at src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/stores/subscriptions.ts:1-18 — currently contains only auto-clear-on-version-change; NO injectSystemMessage/sendValidationFeedback wiring exists yet. 15a2 Task 8 plans the relocation but Phase 3A merges AFTER Phase 2. Constraint is prose-only with no CI gate.
convergence_findings.resolution: Two acceptable paths: (a) PREFERRED — Phase 2C Task 8 only removes the standalone Validate button UI; defer handleValidate/injectSystemMessage/sendValidationFeedback deletion to Phase 3A Task 8 where the relocation is atomic. (b) Pull Phase 3A Task 4+8 into the Phase 2 umbrella PR, expanding scope. Add a mechanical gate either way: a Phase 2C test that imports subscriptions.ts and asserts injectSystemMessage fires on validation failure — fails red until the relocation lands.

convergence_findings.title: secret_service vs scoped_secret_resolver — Protocol matches raw service, app.py precedent points to scoped resolver
convergence_findings.evidence: src/elspeth/web/app.py:469 sets app.state.secret_service = WebSecretService(...) with list_refs(user_id, *, auth_provider_type: AuthProviderType). Line 470 sets app.state.scoped_secret_resolver = ScopedSecretResolver(...) with list_refs(user_id) — no auth_provider_type. ExecutionService (line 232), BlobService, ComposerService all inject scoped_secret_resolver. The plan's _SecretServiceLike Protocol declares list_refs(user_id, *, auth_provider_type: str) which matches ONLY the raw WebSecretService. An implementer following the established 'inject scoped_secret_resolver' precedent will get a TypeError at runtime; MagicMock unit tests do not catch this.
convergence_findings.confidence: HIGH (3 reviewers; reality and quality disagree on which is correct — architecture prefers scoped_secret_resolver to match precedent, reality and the Protocol signature point to raw secret_service; quality recommends matching the established precedent by dropping auth_provider_type from the Protocol)
convergence_findings.resolution: DECIDE EXPLICITLY in the plan, not 'adapt to actual attr name'. RECOMMENDED: match the established codebase precedent — inject app.state.scoped_secret_resolver, drop auth_provider_type from _SecretServiceLike, and have ReadinessService._build_secrets_row call list_refs(user_id) only. Then add an integration test fixture (client_with_session_with_secret_ref) that exercises the real call path (quality REC-1).

blocking_issues.issue: NodeSpec test fixtures will TypeError — 7 of 13 required kwargs missing
blocking_issues.evidence: src/elspeth/web/composer/state.py:129-144. NodeSpec is @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with 13 required fields (id, node_type, plugin, input, on_success, on_error, options, condition, routes, fork_to, branches, policy, merge) and 3 defaulted (trigger, output_mode, expected_output_count). Plan's _state() helpers in test_service.py, test_explain.py, and AuditReadinessPanel.test.tsx (makeComposition) pass 6 args. All tests crash at construction before any assertion runs.
blocking_issues.resolution: Extend every NodeSpec constructor in the plan to pass all 13 required kwargs (on_error=None, condition=None, routes=None, fork_to=None, branches=None, policy=None, merge=None). Co-locate a small NodeSpec factory in a shared test helper to avoid copy-paste rot. Same change needed in 14b's frontend test makeComposition shape.

blocking_issues.issue: OutputSpec test fixtures will TypeError — on_write_failure required field missing
blocking_issues.evidence: src/elspeth/web/composer/state.py:245-268. OutputSpec has 4 required fields (name, plugin, options, on_write_failure) with NO defaults. Plan's _state() helper passes 3.
blocking_issues.resolution: Add on_write_failure='discard' (or appropriate enum/string) to every OutputSpec construction in the plan. Couple with the NodeSpec factory in B1's resolution.

blocking_issues.issue: EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS allowlist has 3 phantom plugin names; 4 real EXTERNAL_CALL plugins are missing
blocking_issues.evidence: grep of src/elspeth/plugins/transforms/ for `name = `: 'llm' EXISTS (transforms/llm/transform.py:1039, determinism=NON_DETERMINISTIC not EXTERNAL_CALL); 'web_fetch' NOT FOUND anywhere; 'web_scrape_extraction' is a module but not a plugin (the plugin name is 'web_scrape' at transforms/web_scrape.py:339); 'dataverse_query' NOT FOUND — Dataverse exists only as the sink named 'dataverse' (sinks/dataverse.py:224). EXTERNAL_CALL transforms not on allowlist: web_scrape, rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield. The plan's own subset-of-catalog test AND the completeness test will both fail on first run.
blocking_issues.resolution: Run the grep the plan prescribes (Task 2 Step 2) BEFORE populating trust.py. Replace allowlist with verified names: 'llm' (NON_DETERMINISTIC, manually curated as LLM-boundary), 'web_scrape', and a deliberate decision for rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield. Document the two-tier rationale: (a) automated coverage via Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL completeness test, (b) manual curation for LLM-class NON_DETERMINISTIC plugins. Drop web_fetch, web_scrape_extraction, dataverse_query.

blocking_issues.issue: api/preferences.ts does not exist — cited 'precedent file' is phantom
blocking_issues.evidence: ls of src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/api/ shows: client.guided.test.ts, client.preferences.test.ts, client.recovery.test.ts, client.ts, websocket.ts. No preferences.ts. Preferences functions (fetchUserComposerPreferences, patchUserComposerPreferences) are inline in client.ts:407+.
blocking_issues.resolution: Update 14b Task 2 Step 1 to reference client.ts:407 (inline preferences functions) as the structural precedent for auditReadiness.ts. The duplication of getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse is real either way — flag that the auditReadiness.ts module is the SECOND extracted API module, not a clone of an existing extraction. Reinforces R1 below (Phase 8 consolidation).

blocking_issues.issue: Phase 2C deletes handleValidate side effects before Phase 3A Task 8 creates the replacement subscription (see C3)
blocking_issues.resolution: See C3 resolution. Preferred: scope-shrink Phase 2C Task 8 to button-removal only; defer handleValidate/injectSystemMessage/sendValidationFeedback deletion to Phase 3A Task 8. Add a CI gate test that imports subscriptions.ts and asserts validation-failure side effects fire.

blocking_issues.issue: affected_nodes producer never wired in validation.py — provenance row's component_ids permanently empty (see C1)
blocking_issues.resolution: See C1 resolution. Update validation.py:1248 in the same commit that adds affected_nodes to ValidationCheck. Add an integration test that exercises the real validate_pipeline path.

blocking_issues.issue: loadSnapshot stale-fetch race — no monotonic write guard (see C2)
blocking_issues.resolution: See C2 resolution. Add monotonic write guard + AbortController cancellation. Add concurrent-fetch test to auditReadinessStore.test.ts.

blocking_issues.issue: error_code/affected_nodes defaults frame deferred call-site updates as backwards-compat shim — violates No-Legacy policy
blocking_issues.evidence: 14a §File structure: 'default keeps existing call sites valid'; plan note 'will produce empty component_ids on old check instances until the advisory code is updated.' CLAUDE.md No-Legacy: 'Change all call sites in the same commit.'
blocking_issues.resolution: Audit every ValidationCheck and ValidationError construction site (grep -rn 'ValidationCheck(' 'ValidationError(' src/ tests/) and pass affected_nodes / error_code values explicitly in the same commit that adds the schema fields. Defaults remain only for fields that are genuinely optional in the domain, not as compat shims. Tie to B6/C1.

blocking_issues.issue: mypy and ruff absent from all 14a commit sequences — CLAUDE.md quality-gate convention violation
blocking_issues.evidence: 14a Tasks 1-4 list pytest and enforce_tier_model.py check but no `mypy src/` or `ruff check src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/`. CLAUDE.md development section: '.venv/bin/python -m mypy src/' and '.venv/bin/python -m ruff check src/' are standard gates.
blocking_issues.resolution: Add `mypy src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/ src/elspeth/web/execution/schemas.py src/elspeth/web/config.py` and `ruff check src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/` to the pre-commit step of each task (or at minimum after Task 5 integration tests pass). New L3 packages must be typed and lint-clean before merge.

See C4 resolution. Decide explicitly: scoped_secret_resolver (matches precedent) is recommended. Drop auth_provider_type from _SecretServiceLike Protocol. Add client_with_session_with_secret_ref integration fixture.

warnings.issue: test_trust.py uses getattr(cls, 'determinism', None) — CLAUDE.md defensive-getattr ban
warnings.evidence: infrastructure/base.py:146 sets determinism on the base class. All plugins inherit it. The None default is never reached. CLAUDE.md unconditionally bans hasattr; getattr-with-default for system-owned plugin attributes is the same defensive pattern.
warnings.resolution: Replace getattr(cls, 'determinism', None) with cls.determinism. If any plugin is missing the attribute, the resulting AttributeError IS the correct crash per the plugin-ownership doctrine. (Quality's anti_patterns_assessment marks this acceptable 'in test context' — architecture's reading aligns with CLAUDE.md and should win the tie.)

warnings.issue: frontend ValidationError interface lacks error_code — type mirror must update in same commit
warnings.evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:310-315 has no error_code field. Backend adds it in Phase 2A. Co-update required to keep wire shape and TS type in sync.
warnings.resolution: Add error_code?: string to ValidationError in types/index.ts as part of Phase 2A (backend) or Phase 2B Task 1 (frontend foundations). Currently absent from the plan's file-structure section.

warnings.issue: getInitialState pattern is NEW, not a mirror of executionStore
warnings.evidence: executionStore.ts:235 defines initialExecutionState as a module-internal const (not exported, not a function). Plan claims auditReadinessStore's exported getInitialState() function mirrors executionStore — incorrect; it's a novel pattern.
warnings.resolution: Remove the 'same pattern used by executionStore' claim. The exported getInitialState() is fine on its merits as a legitimate testability improvement; cite it as such rather than as a mirror.

warnings.issue: validatePipeline line reference off by 28 lines (702 → 730)
warnings.evidence: grep validatePipeline src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/api/client.ts → line 730. Plan 14b Task 2 says 702.
warnings.resolution: Update line reference in 14b Task 2 from 702 to 730.

warnings.issue: Integration test fixture names are entirely novel; existing conftest has none of them
warnings.evidence: tests/integration/web/conftest.py:61-152 defines composer_test_client, session_with_pending_compose_request, session_with_composer_state, inject_commit_OperationalError. None of: client_with_session, client_anonymous, client_with_session_without_state, client_with_user, other_users_session_id, any_session_id.
warnings.resolution: Plan's 'adapt fixture names below' note correctly flags this but understates the work — 6 fixtures must be created. Model on composer_test_client. The IDOR test (cross-user 404 invariant) is the critical security case; it must not be skipped due to missing fixtures.

warnings.issue: Subset-of-catalog tests in tests/unit/ load the full plugin manifest — mislabelled as unit tests
warnings.evidence: test_external_boundary_transforms_subset_of_catalog and test_external_boundary_sinks_subset_of_catalog call PluginManager().register_builtin_plugins() inside tests/unit/. CLAUDE.md: unit tests in tests/unit/, integration in tests/integration/. These tests perform full plugin registration, side effects, and module imports — that's integration behaviour.
warnings.resolution: Move test_trust.py to tests/integration/web/audit_readiness/. Keep the test (it is a genuinely useful build-gate); relocate it. If CI segments by directory, the test currently appears in the fast lane but actually exercises integration behaviour.

warnings.issue: Task 7 compareDocumentPosition assertion brittle to wrapper changes
warnings.evidence: panel.compareDocumentPosition(tablist) === Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING is stable for direct sibling/descendant relationships but can yield DOCUMENT_POSITION_DISCONNECTED if a React fragment wraps the panel.
warnings.resolution: Replace with a role-list-index assertion: query the inspector container, collect all role-qualified descendants, assert panel's index precedes tablist's index. More robust to layout refactors.

warnings.issue: No AbortController on fetches — stale snapshots can land after session navigation
warnings.evidence: Neither fetchAuditReadiness nor loadSnapshot uses AbortController. Session-switch during in-flight fetch lets the response write to the old session's cache.
warnings.resolution: Pass AbortController signal through fetchAuditReadiness. Store per-session AbortController in the store; abort in clearSession and on version change. Partially mitigates C2/B7.

warnings.issue: EXTERNAL_CALL drift detection is unidirectional — missing Determinism marker on a new plugin is silently mis-classified
warnings.evidence: test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted catches allowlist drift; nothing catches a plugin that makes external HTTP/LLM/storage calls but forgot to set determinism=Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL.
warnings.resolution: Document the unidirectionality in trust.py docstring. Phase 7's data_trust_tier ClassVar transition (architecture W2) should replace the marker-based check with a structural one that doesn't depend on author discipline.

warnings.issue: Demo-readiness: panel adds new surface during demo-prep window; 'Audit ready ✓' on incomplete pipeline could mislead
warnings.evidence: Memory project_rc5ux_demo_prep_scope: active program is 'maximize success probability for upcoming tech demo'. All-not_applicable rows collapse to 'Audit ready'. A source-only composition (no sinks) triggers validation=error which DOES expand the panel, but the misread-by-audience risk exists for any all-green state.
warnings.resolution: Consider landing Phase 2A+2B for the demo (panel visible, value delivered) and deferring 2C (Validate-button removal, panel mount in InspectorPanel) until post-demo. Adds the panel without removing a familiar affordance. Operator decision — surface this trade explicitly.

warnings.issue: Three API modules duplicating getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse; Phase 8 deferral may slip
warnings.evidence: 14b Task 2 Step 4 JSDoc acknowledges duplication. Pattern will be duplicated again by any Phase 5b/6/7 API module added before Phase 8.
warnings.resolution: Treat Phase 8 consolidation as load-bearing, not optional. File a filigree blocker that links future API-module phases to the Phase 8 consolidation. Quality and architecture both flagged R1 in their recommendations.

warnings.issue: Phase 3B panel relocation dependency: prose-only constraint with no CI gate on sequence within 15b2 Task 9
warnings.evidence: 14c §Sequencing documents that 15b2 Task 9 Step 4a (relocate panel into siderail-slot-audit-readiness) MUST precede the git rm InspectorPanel step. Step 4a includes a failing test; sequencing within the task must enforce test-before-deletion.
warnings.resolution: Verify 15b2 Task 9 step ordering: panel-renders-in-side-rail test passes BEFORE git rm. The 'CRITICAL' note is present; confirm executor adherence.

recommendations.suggestion: A/B/C split is itself the tracer pattern at coarser granularity — 2A demoable via curl, 2B via vitest, 2C closes loop with staging smoke. No further splitting recommended. Approved as-designed.
Treat Phase 8 auth-helper consolidation as load-bearing. Link from any future API-module phase as a blocker. Avoid a fourth duplicate getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse before Phase 8 ships.

Operator decision: land Phase 2A+2B for the demo; defer Phase 2C (button removal + InspectorPanel mount + sub-components) until post-demo. Delivers ~80% of demo value at lower risk. Preserves familiar Validate button for audience continuity. Surface to operator before final scoping.

Add three test fixtures/cases: (a) client_with_session_with_secret_ref exercising real list_refs call; (b) source-only composition asserting panel does NOT collapse when validation=error; (c) parseResponse error-mapping covered end-to-end through panel rendering.

14b Tasks 1-2 (types + API client) have zero runtime dependency on Phase 2A; can be committed in parallel as soon as 14a Task 1 commits, not after 14a fully merges. Reduces serialization if subplans land on separate branches.

LookupError → 404 paths in snapshot and explain routes use `from None` which suppresses the chain. Per ELSPETH primacy, this is correct (logging is last resort) but for debug visibility a single structlog.debug at the catch site is acceptable. Optional.

Add docstring note to trust.py: 'When Phase 7 adds data_trust_tier: ClassVar, delete this module entirely; do not maintain both.' File a filigree dependency from Phase 7A backend to this deletion. No-Legacy policy requires same-commit replacement, not parallel sources of truth.

what_is_good.structural_approach: Architecture reviewer: structural approach is sound, A/B/C split is appropriate tracer pattern at coarser granularity, no further splitting needed.
what_is_good.layer_compliance: All planned imports are L3-to-L3 or downward (reality N21, architecture pattern_alignment). audit_readiness/ mirrors existing catalog/, execution/, sessions/ package layout cleanly.
what_is_good.tdd_shape: All tasks follow failing-test-first pattern with explicit run commands. Quality reviewer: 'genuine failing-test discipline for all tasks. RED → GREEN → commit structure is explicitly declared and appears followed.'
what_is_good.wire_contract_integrity: ReadinessRowId and ReadinessStatus literals mirrored exactly between backend and frontend. TypeScript exhaustive 'never' arm fails build on divergence (architecture cross_plan_integrity).
what_is_good.security: IDOR 404-not-403 invariant correctly preserved in the ownership extraction. No XSS vectors. User-id scoping from get_current_user is correct (quality security_assessment).
what_is_good.trust_classification_design: Closed-allowlist approach is defensible — auditor can read the explicit list rather than rely on inferred ClassVar. Phase 7 transition path is documented.
what_is_good.placeholder_handoff: 14b ships placeholder ReadinessRowDetail/ExplainDialog that are never mounted (no user-visible regression); 14c replaces in-place. Clean A→B→C boundary.
what_is_good.service_pattern: ReadinessService follows the established service-then-routes pattern (Phase 1A precedent). Constructor injection, Protocol-based dependencies, no Service Locator.

reality.key_signal: Codebase-verified: 5 blockers prove fixtures and allowlist don't match shipping code.

architecture.key_signal: Cross-phase coupling broken (Phase 2C → 3A) and producer-not-wired in validation.py.

quality.key_signal: Signature mismatch hidden by MagicMock; producer dead-on-arrival; missing convention gates.

systems.key_signal: Race condition + cross-phase coupling = silent wrong data and silent capability removal.

next_step: Fix in-session before any implementation begins. Sequence: (1) Run the prescribed greps to populate trust.py with real plugin names (B3); (2) Build a NodeSpec/OutputSpec test factory and update all _state() helpers (B1+B2); (3) Decide secret_service vs scoped_secret_resolver and update Protocol + wiring (B10/C4); (4) Decide handleValidate scope-shrink vs Phase 3A pull-in and add CI gate (B5/C3); (5) Add affected_nodes producer + error_code call-site audit (B6+B8/C1); (6) Add loadSnapshot monotonic write guard + AbortController + concurrent-fetch test (B7/C2); (7) Add mypy/ruff to commit sequences (B9). Then re-run reality and quality reviewers against the revised plan; architecture and systems can be skipped on the second pass unless scope expands. Land Phase 2A+2B for demo if operator agrees; defer Phase 2C to post-demo (R3/W10).
